triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income Ratio
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio is significantly above the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to affordability concerns.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, Tier C applicants must have a DTI ratio of no more than 35%. The applicant's DTI ratio of 440% indicates a severe affordability issue, making the loan unsustainable.

triageFlags.title: Non-Compliant Loan-to-Value Ratio
triageFlags.description: The LTV ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to non-compliance with loan structure policies.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy stipulates a maximum LTV of 95% for Tier C applicants. The current LTV of 91.11% is non-compliant, indicating a risk of over-leverage.

content.reasoning: The applicant's DTI ratio of 440% far exceeds the maximum allowable threshold of 35% for their credit tier, indicating severe affordability concerns. Additionally, the LTV ratio of 91.11% surpasses the maximum permissible limit of 95% for Tier C applicants, further breaching policy guidelines.
